item.title: Building bridges between ideas and code
item.description:
Hi, I’m Roman, a developer who discovered Go one day and realized it was “that one” love at first compile.

My approach is simple: write code that’s understandable not only to the machine but also to the person who visits the repository six months from now (yes, that same me). I’m a fan of clean code, efficient algorithms, and a good cup of coffee while debugging.

Core competencies:

Languages: Go (core, goroutines, channels, net/http)

item.title: Projects
item.description:
If you see a bug or want to improve the functionality, welcome to pull requests.

gostub - lightweight stub server that uses Go templates to generate dynamic API responses, making it easy to simulate external services during development and testing

item.title: Want to discuss a project or just chat over coffee?
item.description:
My code is open to the world, and I’m open to meeting new people. If you have an idea that needs a reliable backend, or just want to discuss the latest news in the world of Go, let me know!

Mail: 1969397@gmail.com

GitHub: github.com/rom4eg (там можно посмотреть, как я пишу код, когда никто не видит)

Telegram: @rom4eg196
